title: Miconia melanodesma
taxonomy: Miconia melanodesma is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The family Melastomataceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the melastome family. This family is further divided into several genera, with Miconia being one of the largest and most widespread. The classification of Miconia melanodesma reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Melastomataceae family.

The leaves and stems of Miconia melanodesma have been traditionally used in folk medicine due to their purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
